title: Practice & Exercises Capstone - Bring It All Together
description: The ultimate Java challenge: 60 real-world exercises combining syntax, variables, operators, conditions, loops, methods, OOP, collections, and string security.
learning_outcomes: Combine all Java concepts into complete, working programs
Design classes with inheritance and polymorphism for real-world scenarios
Use ArrayList and collections to manage dynamic data
Write methods that validate input and handle edge cases securely
Apply string manipulation and security principles in practical contexts
Build multi-class applications with proper OOP structure
Debug complex programs spanning multiple concepts
Refactor messy code into clean, maintainable solutions
Solve interview-style coding challenges with confidence
Create portfolio-worthy Java projects from scratch

ai_tutor_config.personality: Encouraging, patient, uses real-world analogies. Never judges. Celebrates small wins. For capstone exercises, acts as a mentor guiding through multi-step problems.

intervention_triggers.idle_40_seconds: These capstone exercises are bigger than what you have done before - that is expected! Would you like me to break this into smaller steps?
intervention_triggers.idle_90_seconds: Still thinking? That shows dedication! Which part feels most unclear: the classes, the methods, the logic, or the collections?
intervention_triggers.idle_180_seconds: Complex problems take time. Let me walk through step by step. First, let us plan what classes and methods we need.
intervention_triggers.first_error: Good effort! In capstone exercises, errors often come from how parts connect. Let me trace where data flows...
intervention_triggers.repeated_errors: These multi-concept exercises are challenging! Let me show a different approach. Sometimes starting simpler and building up works better.
intervention_triggers.after_success: INCREDIBLE! You just solved a real-world coding challenge combining multiple Java concepts. You should be very proud!

encouragement_phrases: You have come SO far - from Hello World to building complete applications!
This is real programming - combining everything you know to solve problems!
Every professional developer started exactly where you are now!
Take it one method at a time - even big programs are built piece by piece!
Debugging is not failing - it is learning how your code actually works!
You are building portfolio-worthy code right now!
Real coding is 80% thinking, 20% typing - take your time!
You have mastered 10 topics to get here - seriously impressive!
Think of each exercise as a mini-project you can show employers!
The fact you are attempting capstone exercises means you have already learned a lot!

explanation_styles.analogy: Think of a capstone like cooking a full meal. Topics 1-10 taught individual ingredients. Now you combine them into a complete dish.
explanation_styles.visual: Picture a building: syntax is the foundation, conditions are walls, methods are rooms, classes are floors, collections are elevators, and the capstone is the finished building.
explanation_styles.step_by_step: Step 1-Read the problem. Step 2-Identify classes needed. Step 3-Plan methods. Step 4-Write simplest version. Step 5-Add complexity. Step 6-Test each part.
explanation_styles.code_trace: Start at main(). Create objects - what fields? Call methods - what goes in, what comes out? Loop through collections - what happens each iteration?
explanation_styles.comparison: A contacts app uses: Classes for Contact, ArrayList to store them, methods to add/search/delete, loops to display, conditions to validate, strings for names.

lessons.title: The Grand Finale: Combining Everything You Have Learned

theory.introduction: Congratulations - you have made it to the capstone! Think of Topics 1 through 10 as learning individual musical instruments. The capstone is your first concert where you play them all together. In the real world, no program uses just one concept. A simple login system needs: variables (username, password), strings (text manipulation), conditions (checking credentials), methods (organising code), classes (User objects), collections (storing multiple users), loops (searching through users), and operators (comparing values). That is 8 topics in one feature! This capstone has NO new theory - just pure practice. Every exercise combines at least 3 topics. You will build real applications: inventory systems, quiz games, banking simulators, student managers, and more. The exercises start with guided warm-ups combining 2-3 topics, then build to full applications combining 5+ topics. Take your time, use the hints, and remember: if you got here, you already know everything you need.
theory.why_important: In job interviews, employers do not ask you to write a single if-statement. They ask you to build complete solutions. On LeetCode and HackerRank, every challenge combines multiple concepts. The capstone bridges learning individual concepts and being a confident Java programmer. This is where knowledge transforms into skill - and skill is what gets you hired.

key_concepts.explanation: Every real program combines multiple concepts. A to-do list app needs: a Task class (OOP), an ArrayList to store tasks (collections), methods to add/remove/display (methods), a loop to show all tasks (loops), conditions to check if a task exists (conditions), and string formatting for nice output. When you read a problem, think: What classes do I need? What data will they hold? What methods? How will I store multiple objects?
key_concepts.ai_hint: Break capstone exercises into pieces: What objects? What can each do? How to store many of them?

key_concepts.explanation: Break big scary problems into small manageable pieces. 'Build a library system' becomes: Step 1-Book class, Step 2-Library class with ArrayList, Step 3-add/remove/search methods, Step 4-menu loop. Each step is something you already know!
key_concepts.ai_hint: Before writing ANY code, spend 2 minutes planning. Write comments listing the classes, fields, and methods you need.

key_concepts.explanation: Professional programs separate: Model (data classes), View (display/print methods), Controller (main logic/menu). Your classes hold data, display methods show it, menu loop handles interaction. This makes code cleaner and easier to debug.

Always validate input: right type? right range? not empty? no dangerous characters? This combines conditions, methods, loops (to re-ask), and strings. Every real vulnerability starts with unvalidated input.
key_concepts.ai_hint: If a user can type it, they WILL type it wrong. Always validate in a loop.

key_concepts.explanation: If you copy-paste code, extract it into a method or parent class. Do not Repeat Yourself. Methods let you write logic once, inheritance lets children reuse parent code.
key_concepts.ai_hint: If you write the same 3+ lines more than twice, wrap them in a method.

key_concepts.explanation: Almost every capstone uses ArrayList. Key operations: add(), remove(), get(), size(), enhanced for-loop. Combined with conditions: search (loop+if), filter (loop+if+new list), accumulate (loop+sum).
key_concepts.ai_hint: When you see 'manage a list of X', think: ArrayList<X> then plan add, remove, find, display methods.

key_concepts.explanation: Most capstone apps use: show menu -> get choice -> perform action -> repeat. This combines while loop, switch/if, methods, and output. Master this and you can build any text-based application.
key_concepts.ai_hint: Copy this menu template for any exercise that says 'create a system'. Just change options and methods.

key_concepts.explanation: Parent type, several child types. Store all in one ArrayList<Parent>. When you call a method, each object behaves according to its own type. This makes code flexible and extensible.
key_concepts.ai_hint: Different types of same thing = inheritance. Store them all in ArrayList<ParentType>.

key_concepts.explanation: In capstone exercises with user text: trim(), check empty, validate format, never concatenate into commands. Use equalsIgnoreCase() for comparisons. Build these habits now for a secure career.
key_concepts.ai_hint: Treat every external string like a package from an unknown sender - inspect before opening!

key_concepts.explanation: Systematic approach: 1) Compiles? Fix syntax. 2) Crashes? Read exception + line number. 3) Wrong output? Add debug prints. 4) Logic error? Trace manually. 5) Collection issue? Check empty/indices. 6) OOP issue? Check which method is called.
key_concepts.ai_hint: When stuck, add System.out.println("DEBUG: var = " + var) at key points. Like turning on lights in a dark room!

key_concepts.explanation: Spend 3 minutes planning to save 15 minutes debugging. Write your plan as comments: what classes, what fields, what methods, what the main flow looks like. Then fill in code under each comment.
key_concepts.ai_hint: Write your plan as comments FIRST, then code underneath. Comments ARE your roadmap.

key_concepts.explanation: Every capstone exercise is a potential portfolio piece. Keep your best solutions. Put them on GitHub. In interviews, saying 'I built a library management system using OOP, collections, and validation' is powerful.
key_concepts.ai_hint: Write each exercise as if a future employer will read your code - because they might!

Added: Bob

visual_diagram.title: Capstone Problem-Solving Flowchart: From Problem to Solution
visual_diagram.content:
CAPSTONE PROBLEM-SOLVING FLOWCHART

Your step-by-step guide for every capstone exercise

STEP 1: READ & UNDERSTAND
Read the full problem | v NOUNS = Classes (Student, Book, Product) | v VERBS = Methods (add, remove, search, calculate) | v ADJECTIVES = Fields (name, price, active, score)

STEP 2: PLAN YOUR CLASSES
For each class write:
+---------------------------+ | ClassName | +---------------------------+ | Fields: | | - field1 (type) | | - field2 (type) | +---------------------------+ | Methods: | | + method1() | | + method2() | | + toString() | +---------------------------+

STEP 3: DECIDE RELATIONSHIPS
Is there inheritance? | +--- YES: Different TYPES of same thing? | Use: extends | +--- NO: Objects CONTAIN other objects? Use: ArrayList<ChildClass>

STEP 4: BUILD INCREMENTALLY
Phase 1: Simplest version | Create classes + constructors + toString() v Phase 2: Core methods | add(), remove(), find() - test each v Phase 3: Logic & validation | Input checks, edge cases, errors v Phase 4: Polish output Formatted tables, statistics, messages

COMMON PATTERNS TO REUSE
SEARCH: for(T item:list) if(item.getX().equals(target)) return item;
FILTER: ArrayList<T> result=new ArrayList<>(); for(T i:list) if(i.check()) result.add(i);
ACCUMULATE: int total=0; for(T i:list) total+=i.getValue();
FIND MAX: T best=list.get(0); for(T i:list) if(i.getVal()>best.getVal()) best=i;

REMEMBER
1. Every capstone = combination of things you already know
2. Plan first, code second
3. Build simplest version first, then add features
4. Test each piece before connecting
5. You have done 10 topics - YOU CAN DO THIS!

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 1: Personal Profile Printer
practice_exercises.description: Create a Person class with name, age, city. Write getAgeCategory() method using conditions. Display formatted profiles.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Start simple! Classes + conditions + formatted output. Like a social media About Me card.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
===== PROFILE =====
Name:     Alice
Age:      25
City:     London
Category: Adult

hints.message: Constructor: public PersonProfile(String name, int age, String city) { this.name=name; this.age=age; this.city=city; }
getAgeCategory: if (age < 18) return "Young"; else if (age < 65) return "Adult"; else return "Senior";
displayProfile: System.out.println("===== PROFILE ====="); System.out.printf("Name:     %s%n", name); System.out.printf("Age:      %d%n", age); System.out.printf("Category: %s%n", getAgeCategory());

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 2: Temperature Converter
practice_exercises.description: Write static methods for Celsius/Fahrenheit/Kelvin conversions. Display a conversion table using loops and printf.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Build a genuinely useful tool! Methods + operators + loops + formatted output.

hints.message: Use 9.0/5.0 not 9/5 to avoid integer division! celsiusToFahrenheit: return (c * 9.0/5.0) + 32;
Loop: for (int c = 0; c <= 100; c += 10) { System.out.printf("%-10.1f | %-12.1f | %-10.1f%n", (double)c, celsiusToFahrenheit(c), celsiusToKelvin(c)); }
Full methods: public static double celsiusToFahrenheit(double c) { return (c * 9.0/5.0) + 32; } public static double celsiusToKelvin(double c) { return c + 273.15; }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 3: Shopping Receipt
practice_exercises.description: Create Item class (name, price). Store in ArrayList. Calculate subtotal, tax (20%), total. Print formatted receipt.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Everyone understands receipts! Classes + ArrayList + loops + formatted output.

hints.message: Item: class Item { String name; double price; Item(String n, double p){name=n;price=p;} }
Loop: double subtotal=0; for(Item i:cart){System.out.println(i);subtotal+=i.price;}
Tax: double tax=subtotal*0.20; System.out.printf("TOTAL: $%6.2f%n", subtotal+tax);

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 4: Number Guessing Game
practice_exercises.description: Program picks number 1-100, gives higher/lower hints. Track guesses, display performance rating.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Games are the best practice! Loops + conditions + operators + methods.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Guess 1: 50 - Too high!
Guess 2: 25 - Too low!
...
Guess 6: 42 - CORRECT!
Rating: Great job!

hints.message: Loop: for(int i=0; i<guesses.length && !found; i++) { attempts++; if(guesses[i]==secret) found=true; }
Inside loop: if(guesses[i]>secret) print "Too high!"; else if(guesses[i]<secret) print "Too low!"; else { print "CORRECT!"; found=true; }
getRating: if(g<=3) return "Incredible!"; if(g<=6) return "Great job!"; if(g<=10) return "Not bad!"; return "Keep practising!";

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 5: Student Roster Report
practice_exercises.description: Student class with ArrayList<Integer> grades. Methods for average, highest, pass/fail. Display class report.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This exercise hits 6 topics at once! Classes, ArrayList, methods, loops, conditions, formatting.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
===== CLASS REPORT =====
Alice    | Avg: 85.0 | Highest: 92 | PASS
Bob      | Avg: 45.0 | Highest: 52 | FAIL
Class Average: 65.0 | Passed: 1 | Failed: 1

hints.message: Constructor: this.name=name; this.grades=new ArrayList<>(); addGrade: if(g>=0&&g<=100) grades.add(g);
getAverage: int sum=0; for(int g:grades) sum+=g; return (double)sum/grades.size();
In main: int passed=0; for(Student s:students){System.out.println(s); if(s.isPassing()) passed++;}

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 6: Word Counter & Analyser
practice_exercises.description: Count words, vowels, find longest/shortest word in a text. Each analysis as its own method.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: String processing meets methods and loops! Build a text analysis tool step by step.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Word count: 6
Vowel count: 15
Longest: programming
Reversed: egaugnal gnimmargorp lufrewop a si avaJ

hints.message: countWords: return text.trim().split("\\s+").length;
countVowels: int c=0; for(char ch:text.toLowerCase().toCharArray()) if("aeiou".indexOf(ch)>=0) c++; return c;
reverseText: return new StringBuilder(text).reverse().toString();

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 7: Simple Inventory
practice_exercises.description: Product class (name,price,stock). sell() with validation. Display inventory report with total value.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: CRUD operations - backbone of real applications! Create, Read, Update, Display.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Keyboard    | $49.99 | Stock: 20 | Value: $999.80
...
Total value: $5244.15
Sold 5 Keyboards: SUCCESS
Sold 50 Mouse: FAILED (only 45 in stock)

hints.message: sell: if(qty>0 && qty<=stock) { stock-=qty; return true; } return false;
getValue: return price * stock;
Loop: double total=0; for(Product p:inv){System.out.println(p);total+=p.getValue();}

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 8: Pattern Printer
practice_exercises.description: Methods for triangle, box, and number triangle patterns using nested loops.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Classic interview question! Nested loops + methods. Start with the simplest pattern.

hints.message: Triangle: outer loop rows, inner: (rows-1-i) spaces then (2*i+1) stars.
Box: if(i==0||i==size-1) full stars, else star+(size-2)spaces+star.
NumberTriangle: for(int i=1;i<=rows;i++){for(int j=1;j<=i;j++) System.out.print(j+" "); System.out.println();}

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 9: Unit Converter
practice_exercises.description: Static methods for km/miles and kg/lbs. Display conversion tables with loops and printf.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Utility classes are workhorses of professional code! Clean methods, formatted output.

hints.message: Each method is one line: public static double kmToMiles(double km) { return km * 0.621371; }
Distance loop: for(int i=1;i<=10;i++) System.out.printf("%-6.1f|%-6.1f%n",(double)i,kmToMiles(i));
Weight loop: for(int i=10;i<=100;i+=10) System.out.printf("%-6.1f|%-6.1f%n",(double)i,kgToLbs(i));

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 10: Dice Simulator
practice_exercises.description: Roll two dice 100 times. Track frequency of sums 2-12. Display histogram.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Combine random, arrays, loops, formatted output! See probability in action.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Sum |Count| Bar
  2 |   3 | ###
  7 |  17 | #################
 12 |   2 | ##
(Numbers vary - 7 appears most!)

hints.message: Rolling: for(int i=0;i<100;i++){ int sum=rollDie()+rollDie(); freq[sum]++; }
Display: for(int i=2;i<=12;i++) System.out.printf("%3d|%4d | %s%n",i,freq[i],"#".repeat(freq[i]));
Sum 7 has the most combinations (1+6,2+5,3+4,4+3,5+2,6+1) so it appears most often.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 11: Vehicle Fleet Manager
practice_exercises.description: Create Vehicle parent, Car/Truck/Motorcycle children. Each calculates fuel cost differently. Fleet report from ArrayList.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This combines multiple OOP concepts. Plan your classes first, then build one method at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Toyota Corolla (Car) -> $50.00
Volvo FH (Truck) -> $133.93
Honda CB (Motorcycle) -> $25.00
Cheapest: Honda CB

hints.message: Car: return (km/efficiency)*price; Truck: return (km/(efficiency*0.7))*price; Motorcycle: (km/(efficiency*1.2))*price
Store all in ArrayList<Vehicle>. Loop to display and find cheapest (track min cost).
class Car extends Vehicle { Car(String n,double e){super(n,e);} String getType(){return"Car";} double fuelCost(double km,double p){return(km/efficiency)*p;} }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 12: Pet Shelter
practice_exercises.description: Dog/Cat/Rabbit extend Pet. Each has unique speak(). Track adopted vs available.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This combines multiple OOP concepts. Plan your classes first, then build one method at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Rex adopted by Alice! Says: Woof!
Available: Whiskers(Cat), Bella(Dog)
Adopted: Rex(Dog), Mittens(Cat)

hints.message: class Dog extends Pet { Dog(String n,int a){super(n,a);} String speak(){return"Woof!";} String getType(){return"Dog";} }
showAvailable: for(Pet p:pets) if(!p.adopted) System.out.println(p.name+"("+p.getType()+")");
adoptPet: loop through, find by name(equalsIgnoreCase), call adopt(owner).

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 13: Bank Account System
practice_exercises.description: SavingsAccount(interest) and CurrentAccount(overdraft) extending BankAccount.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This combines multiple OOP concepts. Plan your classes first, then build one method at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Alice deposits $500: SUCCESS
Interest applied: $75.00
Bob overdraft withdraw $800: SUCCESS ($-300.00)

hints.message: SavingsAccount: applyInterest() { double i=balance*rate; balance+=i; }
CurrentAccount: override withdraw to check amount<=balance+odLimit
class SavingsAccount extends BankAccount { double rate; SavingsAccount(String h,double b,double r){super(h,b);rate=r;} void applyInterest(){balance+=balance*rate;} }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 14: Shape Area Competition
practice_exercises.description: Circle/Rectangle/Triangle extending Shape. Find largest area from mixed ArrayList.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This combines multiple OOP concepts. Plan your classes first, then build one method at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Circle(r=5): Area=78.54
Rectangle(4x6): Area=24.00
WINNER: Circle with area 78.54

hints.message: Circle area: Math.PI*r*r. Rectangle: w*h. Triangle: 0.5*base*height.
Find largest: Shape biggest=shapes.get(0); for(Shape s:shapes) if(s.getArea()>biggest.getArea()) biggest=s;
abstract class Shape { abstract double getArea(); abstract String getName(); public String toString(){return getName()+" Area:"+String.format("%.2f",getArea());} }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 15: Employee Payroll
practice_exercises.description: Manager(15% bonus+team), Developer(10% bonus), Intern(capped at 30k). Payroll report.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This combines multiple OOP concepts. Plan your classes first, then build one method at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Alice|Manager|$86,500.00
Bob|Developer|$77,000.00
Diana|Intern|$28,000.00
Total: $287,500.00

hints.message: Manager: baseSalary*1.15 + teamSize*500. Developer: baseSalary*1.10. Intern: Math.min(baseSalary, 30000).
Manager needs teamSize field: Manager(String n, double s, int t) { super(n,s); teamSize=t; }
class Manager extends Employee { int ts; Manager(String n,double s,int t){super(n,s);ts=t;} double calculatePay(){return baseSalary*1.15+ts*500;} String getRole(){return"Manager";} }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 16: Music Playlist
practice_exercises.description: Song(title,artist,duration). Playlist with add, remove, findByArtist, totalDuration.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This combines multiple OOP concepts. Plan your classes first, then build one method at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Bohemian Rhapsody | Queen | 5:55
Imagine | John Lennon | 3:01
Total: 5 songs | Duration: 30:39

hints.message: getFormatted: return String.format("%d:%02d", seconds/60, seconds%60);
findByArtist: loop, add to new ArrayList if artist matches equalsIgnoreCase.
getTotalDuration: int total=0; for(Song s:songs) total+=s.getSeconds(); return total;

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 17: Zoo Animal Registry
practice_exercises.description: Mammal(fur)/Bird(wingspan)/Reptile(scales) extending Animal. Census with category counts.

Leo|Lion|Age:5|Mammal|Fur: Golden mane
Polly|Parrot|Age:3|Bird|Wingspan: 0.8m

hints.message: class Mammal extends Animal { String fur; Mammal(String n,String s,int a,String f){super(n,s,a);fur=f;} String getCategory(){return"Mammal";} String getSpecialInfo(){return"Fur: "+fur;} }
Count categories: int m=0,b=0,r=0; for(Animal a:animals) switch(a.getCategory()){case"Mammal":m++;break;...}
Display: System.out.printf("%-12s|%-12s|Age:%2d|%s|%s%n",name,species,age,getCategory(),getSpecialInfo());

Soup: $5.99

Subtotal: $24.98 | Tax: $5.00 | Total: $29.98

hints.message: getSubtotal: double sum=0; for(MenuItem m:items) sum+=m.price; return sum;
getTax: return getSubtotal() * 0.20; getTotal: return getSubtotal() + getTax();
Daily summary: loop all orders, sum getTotals. Count items by category for popularity.

...

hints.message: getTotalCalories: int sum=0; for(Workout w:workouts) sum+=w.calories; return sum;
getMostActiveDay: track day with highest calories across all workouts.
Average: System.out.printf("Avg: %.1f min/day | %.1f cal/day%n", (double)totalMin/7, (double)totalCal/7);

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 20: Online Store Cart
practice_exercises.description: Product catalogue, ShoppingCart, discount codes, checkout receipt.

practice_exercises.expected_output:
Java Book x2: $79.98
USB Drive x3: $44.97
Subtotal: $214.94

TOTAL: $193.45

hints.message: applyDiscount: if(code.equals("SAVE10")) discount=0.10; else if(code.equals("SAVE20")) discount=0.20;
addItem: check stock first! if(product.stock>=qty) { items.add(new CartItem(product,qty)); product.sell(qty); }
checkout: double sub=getSubtotal(); double disc=sub*discount; System.out.printf("TOTAL: $%.2f%n",sub-disc);

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 21: Contact Directory
practice_exercises.description: Add, search by name/phone, remove, display. Handle duplicates and case-insensitive search.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Collections are the backbone of real apps! Plan your methods, code one at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Added: Alice
Duplicate rejected: Alice
Search 'bob': Bob|555-0202
Removed: Alice

hints.message: Duplicate check: for(Contact c:contacts) if(c.name.equalsIgnoreCase(name)) { print rejected; return; }
searchByName: for(Contact c:contacts) if(c.name.equalsIgnoreCase(name.trim())) return c; return null;
remove: contacts.removeIf(c -> c.name.equalsIgnoreCase(name));

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 22: Leaderboard
practice_exercises.description: Player(name,score). Sort by score descending. Show ranks with gold/silver/bronze.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Collections are the backbone of real apps! Plan your methods, code one at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
#1 Alice 950 *** GOLD ***
#2 Charlie 880 ** SILVER **
#3 Eve 820 * BRONZE *
#4 Bob 750

hints.message: Selection sort: find max in unsorted portion, swap to front, repeat.
for(int i=0;i<players.size()-1;i++){int mx=i;for(int j=i+1;j<players.size();j++)if(players.get(j).score>players.get(mx).score)mx=j; swap i and mx; }
Medal: String medal = i==0?"*** GOLD ***":i==1?"** SILVER **":i==2?"* BRONZE *":"";

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 23: Movie Database
practice_exercises.description: Movie(title,director,year,rating). Filter by year range, find highest rated, search by director.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Collections are the backbone of real apps! Plan your methods, code one at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
The Matrix|Wachowskis|1999|8.7
Highest: Pulp Fiction (8.9)
Nolan Films: Inception(8.8), Interstellar(8.6)

hints.message: filterByYear: ArrayList<Movie> r=new ArrayList<>(); for(Movie m:movies) if(m.year>=from&&m.year<=to) r.add(m); return r;
findHighestRated: Movie best=movies.get(0); for(Movie m:movies) if(m.rating>best.rating) best=m; return best;
searchByDirector: loop, add to result if director.equalsIgnoreCase(d).

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 24: Task Priority Queue
practice_exercises.description: Tasks with priority 1-5. Process highest priority first. Display sorted by priority.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Collections are the backbone of real apps! Plan your methods, code one at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
[P5] Fix security bug [PENDING]
[P5] Deploy hotfix [DONE]
[P3] Write docs [PENDING]
Pending: 4 | Completed: 3

hints.message: getNextTask: loop to find highest priority where !done. Track best priority and task.
completeNext: Task next=getNextTask(); if(next!=null) { next.complete(); print("Completed: "+next.title); }
Sort: selection sort comparing priority values, highest first.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 25: Grade Book Statistics
practice_exercises.description: Store students+grades. Class average, top student, students needing help (below average).

practice_exercises.expected_output:
Alice|Avg:85.0|PASS
Bob|Avg:45.0|FAIL
Class Avg: 73.7 | Top: Charlie(91.0)
Need support: Bob(45.0)

hints.message: getClassAverage: sum all student averages / student count.
getTopStudent: Student top=students.get(0); for(Student s:students) if(s.getAverage()>top.getAverage()) top=s;
getStudentsNeedingHelp: double ca=getClassAverage(); for(Student s:students) if(s.getAverage()<ca) result.add(s);

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 26: Recipe Scaler
practice_exercises.description: Ingredient(name,amount,unit). Recipe scales all ingredients proportionally by servings.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Collections are the backbone of real apps! Plan your methods, code one at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
=== Pasta (4 servings) ===
400.0 g spaghetti
=== Pasta (2 servings) ===
200.0 g spaghetti

hints.message: Scale factor: double factor = (double)targetServings / baseServings;
displayForServings: for(Ingredient i:ingredients) System.out.println(i.scaled(factor));
addIngredient: ingredients.add(new Ingredient(name, amount, unit));

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 27: Bookmark Manager
practice_exercises.description: Bookmark(title,url,tag). Search by tag, partial title match, display grouped by tag.

practice_exercises.expected_output:
[Programming]
Java Docs - docs.oracle.com
GitHub - github.com
[Reference]
Wikipedia - wikipedia.org
Total: 8 bookmarks in 4 categories

hints.message: searchByTitle partial: if(b.title.toLowerCase().contains(search.toLowerCase()))
displayGrouped: collect unique tags first, then nested loop printing matches.
Unique tags: ArrayList<String> tags=new ArrayList<>(); for(Bookmark b:bookmarks) if(!tags.contains(b.tag)) tags.add(b.tag);

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 28: Event Calendar
practice_exercises.description: Event(title,date,time,location). Filter by date, check for time conflicts.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Collections are the backbone of real apps! Plan your methods, code one at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
2025-03-15|09:00|Team Meeting|Room 101
Conflict: 2025-03-15 14:00 -> CONFLICT with Code Review!

hints.message: getByDate: loop, add to result if date matches.
hasConflict: for(Event e:events) if(e.date.equals(n.date)&&e.time.equals(n.time)) return true; return false;
Display: System.out.printf("%s|%s|%-20s|%s%n",date,time,title,location);

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 29: Survey Analyser
practice_exercises.description: SurveyQuestion with responses(1-5). Average per question, distribution bar chart.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Collections are the backbone of real apps! Plan your methods, code one at a time.

hints.message: addResponse: if(r>=1&&r<=5) responses.add(r);
getDistribution: int[] dist=new int[6]; for(int r:responses) dist[r]++; return dist;
Bar: for(int i=1;i<=5;i++) System.out.printf("%d: %-10s (%d)%n",i,"#".repeat(dist[i]),dist[i]);

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 30: Flashcard Quiz
practice_exercises.description: Flashcard(question,answer,timesCorrect,timesWrong). Run quiz, track weak cards for review.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Collections are the backbone of real apps! Plan your methods, code one at a time.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Q: Default int value? -> 1 -> WRONG! (Answer: 0)
Score: 4/6 (67%)
Cards to review: Default int value (0%)

hints.message: Check: if(answer.equalsIgnoreCase(card.answer.trim())) card.recordCorrect(); else card.recordWrong();
getWeakCards: filter where getSuccessRate() < 70.
getSuccessRate: if(correct+wrong==0)return 0; return (double)correct/(correct+wrong)*100;

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 31: Email Validator
practice_exercises.description: Check @, domain with dot, no spaces, not empty. Test with 8 emails.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.

hints.message: Start easy: if(email==null||email.isEmpty()||email.contains(" ")) return false;
int at=email.indexOf('@'); if(at<1||at!=email.lastIndexOf('@')) return false; String domain=email.substring(at+1);
Check domain: if(!domain.contains(".")||domain.startsWith(".")||domain.endsWith(".")) return false; return true;

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 32: Password Generator
practice_exercises.description: Generate random passwords with uppercase, lowercase, digits, special chars. Check strength.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Length 8: Ax3$kM9! MODERATE
Length 12: bN7#pQ2&wR4$ STRONG
(Actual vary due to randomness)

hints.message: Random char: String all=UPPER+LOWER+DIGITS+SPECIAL; char c=all.charAt((int)(Math.random()*all.length()));
Guarantee types: first pick one from each pool, then fill remaining randomly.
checkStrength: count criteria met (hasUpper, hasLower, hasDigit, hasSpecial, length>=8, length>=12). Score 0-6.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 33: Input Sanitiser
practice_exercises.description: Remove HTML tags, detect SQL/script injection, trim, limit length.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Hello
[BLOCKED: Script injection]
[BLOCKED: SQL injection]
spaces
very long text that is truncat

hints.message: detectInjection: check lowercase contains "script", "drop table", "select *", "1=1".
removeHtml: return input.replaceAll("<[^>]*>","");
sanitise: if(detectInjection(s)) return blocked; s=s.trim(); s=removeHtml(s); if(s.length()>max) s=s.substring(0,max); return s;

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 34: Caesar Cipher
practice_exercises.description: Encode and decode by shifting letters. Brute force all 26 shifts on mystery text.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Encoded: Khoor Zruog
Decoded: Hello World

hints.message: For each char: if(Character.isUpperCase(c)) newChar=(char)((c-'A'+shift)%26+'A');
Decode = encode with 26-shift: decode(text,shift) { return encode(text,26-shift); }

StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder(); for(char c:text.toCharArray()){ if(Character.isUpperCase(c)) sb.append((char)((c-'A'+shift)%26+'A')); else if(Character.isLowerCase(c)) sb.append((char)((c-'a'+shift)%26+'a')); else sb.append(c); }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 35: Credit Card Validator
practice_exercises.description: Luhn algorithm, card type detection (Visa=4,MC=5), number masking.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
****0366|Visa|VALID
****9903|Mastercard|VALID
****3456|Unknown|INVALID

hints.message: Luhn: double every second digit from right. If>9 subtract 9. Sum all. Valid if sum%10==0.
getCardType: starts with '4' and length 16 -> Visa. '5' and 16 -> Mastercard.
mask: return "****"+num.substring(num.length()-4);

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 36: Hangman Game
practice_exercises.description: Display word with guessed/unguessed letters. Track wrong guesses. Win/lose conditions.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
_ A _ A Wrong:0
_ A _ A Wrong:1
J A _ A Wrong:1
J A V A Wrong:1
YOU WIN!

hints.message: getDisplay: StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder(); for(char c:word.toCharArray()) sb.append(guessed.contains(c)?c+" ":"_ ");
guess: guessed.add(letter); if(!word.contains(String.valueOf(letter))) wrong++;
isWon: for(char c:word.toCharArray()) if(!guessed.contains(c)) return false; return true;

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 37: URL Parser
practice_exercises.description: Parse URL into protocol, domain, path, query. Validate components.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Protocol: https
Domain: example.com
Path: /page
Query: id=1
Valid: YES

hints.message: Split protocol: int idx=url.indexOf("://"); if(idx>0) protocol=url.substring(0,idx);
After removing protocol+://, split at first / for domain vs rest. Split rest at ? for path vs query.
isValid: return protocol!=null && !protocol.isEmpty() && domain!=null && domain.contains(".");

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 38: Palindrome Checker
practice_exercises.description: Check words, sentences (ignore spaces/punctuation), numbers. Find palindromes in a sentence.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
racecar: YES
A man a plan a canal Panama: YES
Palindromes found: Bob, kayak, racecar

hints.message: isPalindrome: return new StringBuilder(text).reverse().toString().equalsIgnoreCase(text);
isSentencePalindrome: String clean=text.replaceAll("[^a-zA-Z]","").toLowerCase(); return isPalindrome(clean);
findPalindromes: split into words, check each, collect those that are palindromes and length>1.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 39: Text Encryption (Substitution)
practice_exercises.description: Create a substitution cipher with a custom alphabet mapping. Encode and decode messages.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Encoded: svool dliow
Decoded: hello world

hints.message: encode: for each char, find its index in plain, replace with char at same index in cipher.
decode: for each char, find its index in cipher, replace with char at same index in plain.

StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder(); for(char c:text.toCharArray()){ int idx=plain.indexOf(Character.toLowerCase(c)); if(idx>=0) sb.append(Character.isUpperCase(c)?Character.toUpperCase(cipher.charAt(idx)):cipher.charAt(idx)); else sb.append(c); }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 40: Username Validator
practice_exercises.description: Check username rules: 3-20 chars, starts with letter, only letters/digits/underscore, no consecutive underscores.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Security starts with proper string handling! Every check you learn here prevents real vulnerabilities.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
alice_99 -> VALID
_bob -> INVALID
ab -> INVALID
test__user -> INVALID
Hello! -> INVALID

hints.message: Check length: if(u.length()<3||u.length()>20) return false;
Starts with letter: if(!Character.isLetter(u.charAt(0))) return false;
Only allowed chars: for(char c:u.toCharArray()) if(!Character.isLetterOrDigit(c)&&c!='_') return false; No consecutive __: if(u.contains("__")) return false;

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 41: Fix the Messy Code
practice_exercises.description: Given badly written code with no methods, poor naming, and no validation. Refactor into clean OOP.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Alice | Avg: 85.0 | PASS
Bob | Avg: 45.0 | FAIL
Class Average: 65.0

hints.message: Step 1: Create a Student class with name and ArrayList<Integer> grades.
Step 2: Add methods getAverage(), isPassing(), toString() to the Student class.
Step 3: In main, create ArrayList<Student>, add students, loop to display. Add class average calculation.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 42: Eliminate Duplicate Code
practice_exercises.description: Three nearly identical methods for processing staff, customers, and suppliers. Extract common logic into a base class.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
=== STAFF ===
Alice - Manager
Total: 3
=== CUSTOMERS ===
Dave - dave@mail.com
Total: 2

hints.message: Create a generic method: static void printSection(String title, ArrayList<String> entries)
Or create a Person class with name + detail fields, and a typed ArrayList<Person>.

static void printSection(String title, ArrayList<String> entries) { System.out.println("=== "+title+" ==="); for(String e:entries) System.out.println(e); System.out.println("Total: "+entries.size()); }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 43: Add Validation to Unsafe Code
practice_exercises.description: Given code that crashes on bad input. Add null checks, range validation, and error messages.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
ERROR: Cannot divide by zero
ERROR: Score must be 0-100
ERROR: Invalid name
ERROR: Name cannot be null

hints.message: divide: if(b==0) { System.out.println("ERROR: Cannot divide by zero"); return 0; }
getGrade: if(score<0||score>100) { System.out.println("ERROR: Score must be 0-100"); return '?'; }
getInitials: if(name==null) return "ERROR: null"; if(name.trim().isEmpty()||!name.contains(" ")) return "ERROR: Invalid name";

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 44: Convert Procedural to OOP
practice_exercises.description: Rewrite a flat, procedural program as proper OOP with classes, encapsulation, and methods.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Java Basics by Smith [AVAILABLE]
OOP Guide by Jones [OUT: Alice]
Security 101 by Brown [AVAILABLE]
Available: 2/3

hints.message: Step 1: Create Book class with all four fields and checkout()/returnBook() methods.
Step 2: Create Library class with ArrayList<Book> and methods for display, checkout, count.
Step 3: Book handles its own state (checkout sets available=false, borrower=name). Library manages the collection.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 45: Improve Error Messages
practice_exercises.description: Given code with generic error messages. Make them specific, helpful, and user-friendly.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
ERROR: Username cannot be null. Please provide a username.
ERROR: Username cannot be empty. Please enter your username.
ERROR: Password must be at least 8 characters (yours has 5).
SUCCESS: Login accepted!

hints.message: Replace each generic "Error" with a specific message telling the user WHAT is wrong and HOW to fix it.
Include the actual value where helpful: "Password has " + pass.length() + " characters, minimum is 8."
Good pattern: System.out.println("ERROR: [what's wrong]. [how to fix]."); return false;

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 46: Add toString and Formatting
practice_exercises.description: Given classes that print ugly output. Add proper toString(), printf formatting, and aligned columns.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.

hints.message: toString: return String.format("%-16s| %-11s| $%,11.2f", name, dept, salary);
Header: System.out.printf("%-16s| %-11s| %s%n", "NAME", "DEPT", "SALARY");
Total: double sum=0; for(Employee e:team) sum+=e.salary; System.out.printf("Total: $%,.2f | Average: $%,.2f%n",sum,sum/team.size());

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 47: Extract Methods from Long Main
practice_exercises.description: Given a 50-line main method. Break it into well-named methods with clear responsibilities.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Sum: 583
Average: 58.3
Max: 93
Min: 17
Above average: 5

hints.message: Extract each block: static int getSum(ArrayList<Integer> nums) { int sum=0; for(int n:nums) sum+=n; return sum; }
Each method takes ArrayList<Integer> and returns one value. Main becomes 5 clean method calls.
static int getMax(ArrayList<Integer> nums) { int max=nums.get(0); for(int n:nums) if(n>max) max=n; return max; }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 48: Fix Security Vulnerabilities
practice_exercises.description: Code with injection risks, no input validation, and information leakage. Fix all issues.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
SAFE query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?
Parameter: Alice

BLOCKED: Input contains suspicious characters!
'; DROP TABLE users; -- was rejected

hints.message: Never concatenate user input into queries. Show parameterised concept: use ? placeholders.
Error messages: remove server IP, database name, stack traces. Show generic message to user, log details internally.
buildQuery fix: if(input.contains("'")||input.contains(";")||input.contains("--")) return "BLOCKED"; Return "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?" + "\nParameter: "+input;

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 49: Optimise Inefficient Code
practice_exercises.description: Given code with unnecessary loops, redundant calculations, and poor data structures. Optimise it.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Has duplicate: true
Total: 150

hints.message: containsDuplicate fix: for(int i=0;i<list.size();i++) for(int j=i+1;j<list.size();j++) if(list.get(i).equals(list.get(j))) return true; return false;
Or simpler: check if indexOf != lastIndexOf: return list.indexOf(item) != list.lastIndexOf(item);
display fix: int total=0; for(int p:prices) { System.out.println("$"+p); total+=p; } System.out.println("Total: $"+total);

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 50: Add Inheritance to Flat Classes
practice_exercises.description: Given three similar classes with copied code. Refactor using a parent class and inheritance.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: Refactoring is a key professional skill! Take messy code and make it clean, secure, and maintainable.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Rex is eating kibble | Woof!
Whiskers is eating tuna | Meow!
Rex is sleeping
Whiskers is sleeping

hints.message: Extract common: abstract class Pet { String name; int age; String owner; void sleep(){...} String getInfo(){...} abstract void eat(); abstract void makeSound(); }
Dog extends Pet: only override eat() and makeSound(). All shared code lives in Pet.

abstract class Pet { String name;int age;String owner; Pet(String n,int a,String o){name=n;age=a;owner=o;} void sleep(){System.out.println(name+" is sleeping");} abstract void eat(); abstract void makeSound(); }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 51: Student Management System
practice_exercises.description: Complete student CRUD: add, search, update grade, remove, display sorted by average. Class statistics.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
===== STUDENT REPORT =====
S001|Alice|Avg:91.0|PASS
S002|Bob|Avg:85.0|PASS
S005|Eve|Avg:45.0|FAIL
---
Class Avg: 73.2 | Pass Rate: 83%
Top 3: Alice(91), Bob(85), Charlie(78)

hints.message: addStudent: check for duplicate ID first. for(Student s:students) if(s.getId().equals(id)) reject.
getTopStudents: sort by average descending (selection sort), return first n students.
getClassStatistics: loop all students to get sum of averages, count passing, find min/max.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 52: Library System
practice_exercises.description: Book checkout/return, member management, overdue tracking, search by title/author.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
=== CATALOGUE ===
Clean Code|R.Martin|[AVAILABLE]
Design Patterns|GoF|[OUT: Alice]
---
Alice's books: Design Patterns
Available: 3/4

hints.message: checkoutBook: find book by isbn, find member by id, call book.checkout(member.name), member.borrowBook(title).
searchByTitle: loop books, if title.toLowerCase().contains(search.toLowerCase()) add to results.
Two-way tracking: Book knows its borrower, Member knows their books. Both must be updated on checkout/return.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 53: Banking Application
practice_exercises.description: Multiple account types, transfer between accounts, transaction history, balance report.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
=== BALANCES ===
SAV001|Alice|$1,575.00|Savings(5%)
CUR001|Bob|$-100.00|Current(OD:$500)

=== Alice's History ===
Deposit: $500.00 -> $1,500.00
Interest: $75.00 -> $1,575.00

Transfer: Bob->Alice $200: SUCCESS

hints.message: Transaction history: after each operation, history.add("Deposit: $"+amount+" -> $"+balance);
transfer: Account from=findAccount(fromId); if(from.withdraw(amount)) { to.deposit(amount); } else print failed.
SavingsAccount withdraw: if(balance-amount < minBalance) reject. CurrentAccount: allow up to balance+overdraftLimit.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 54: Quiz Game Engine
practice_exercises.description: Question bank, random selection, multiple difficulty levels, score tracking, review wrong answers.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
=== JAVA QUIZ (5 questions, INTERMEDIATE) ===
Q1: What does ArrayList.size() return? -> 2 -> CORRECT!
...
Score: 4/5 (80%)

--- REVIEW ---
Q3: What is polymorphism? (Your answer: 1, Correct: 3)
Explanation: Polymorphism means...

hints.message: getQuestionsByDifficulty: filter allQuestions by difficulty field. Return matching ArrayList.
runQuiz: select questions, loop through with simulated answers, track score and wrong answers in QuizResult.
displayReview: for(Question q:result.wrongAnswers) { q.display(); print correct answer and explanation; }

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 55: Expense Tracker
practice_exercises.description: Expense(description,amount,category,date). Monthly summary, category breakdown, budget tracking.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
=== MONTHLY EXPENSE REPORT ===
Food:     $450.00 (22.5%)
Transport: $200.00 (10.0%)
Entertain: $150.00 (7.5%)
---
Total: $1,800.00 / Budget: $2,000.00
Remaining: $200.00 | Status: UNDER BUDGET

hints.message: getTotalByCategory: double sum=0; for(Expense e:expenses) if(e.category.equals(cat)) sum+=e.amount; return sum;
getCategoryBreakdown: collect unique categories, for each get total and percentage of grand total.
isOverBudget: return getMonthTotal() > monthlyBudget;

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 56: Movie Recommendation System
practice_exercises.description: Rate movies, find similar taste users, recommend unwatched movies based on ratings.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
Alice and Bob share 3 movies. Similarity: 85%
Recommendations for Alice:
  Inception (rated 5 by Bob)
  Interstellar (rated 4 by Charlie)

hints.message: getCommonMovies: for each of user1's movies, check if user2.hasRated(title). Collect common titles.
getSimilarity: for common movies, calculate average absolute difference. Lower diff = higher similarity.
getRecommendations: find most similar user, return their high-rated movies that target user hasn't seen.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 57: Simple Chat Message System
practice_exercises.description: User(name), Message(sender,text,timestamp). Chat room with message history, search, word count.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
=== General Chat (3 members) ===
[10:00] Alice: Hello everyone!
[10:01] Bob: Hi Alice!
[10:02] Alice: How is the Java project going?
---
Total messages: 5
Alice's messages: 3
Search 'Java': 1 result

hints.message: sendMessage: messages.add(new Message(sender, text, timestamp));
searchMessages: ArrayList<Message> results=new ArrayList<>(); for(Message m:messages) if(m.text.toLowerCase().contains(keyword.toLowerCase())) results.add(m);
getMessagesByUser: filter messages where sender.equalsIgnoreCase(name).

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 58: Fitness Challenge App
practice_exercises.description: Users set goals, log workouts, track streaks, earn badges, leaderboard by total activity.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
=== LEADERBOARD ===
#1 Alice: 2500 cal | 5 workouts | Badges: [Streak3, CalorieBurner]
#2 Bob: 1800 cal | 4 workouts | Badges: [FirstWorkout]

Alice's Goals:
- Run 10km: 8/10 km (80%) IN PROGRESS
- Burn 3000cal: 2500/3000 (83%) IN PROGRESS

hints.message: getTotalCalories: int sum=0; for(WorkoutLog w:logs) sum+=w.calories; return sum;
checkGoals: for each goal, if current >= target, set achieved=true and award badge.
Leaderboard: sort participants by total calories descending.

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 59: Text Adventure Game
practice_exercises.description: Rooms, items, player inventory. Move between rooms, pick up/drop items, win condition.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.expected_output:
You are in the Library. A dusty room full of books.
Items here: Old Key, Java Manual
> take Old Key
Picked up: Old Key
> go north
You are in the Corridor. A long dark hallway.
> inventory
You are carrying: Old Key

hints.message: Room connections: room1.north = room2; room2.south = room1; (bidirectional links)
move: if(direction=="north"&&currentRoom.north!=null) currentRoom=currentRoom.north; else print("Can't go that way");
pickUp: for(Item i:currentRoom.items) if(i.name.equalsIgnoreCase(itemName)){inventory.add(i);currentRoom.items.remove(i);break;}

practice_exercises.title: Exercise 60: Portfolio Capstone - Build Your Dream App
practice_exercises.description: Design and build a complete application of YOUR choice using ALL topics. This is your portfolio showpiece!
practice_exercises.ai_intro: This is a complete application project! Plan your classes first, build incrementally, test each piece.
practice_exercises.starter_code:
/**
 * CAPSTONE PORTFOLIO PROJECT
 * 
 * Design and build a complete Java application that demonstrates:
 * 1. At least 3 classes (with at least 1 using inheritance)
 * 2. ArrayList for data storage
 * 3. At least 5 methods per main class
 * 4. Input validation
 * 5. Formatted output with printf
 * 6. String manipulation
 * 7. Conditions and loops working together
 * 8. Encapsulation (private fields, public methods)
 * 
 * IDEAS:
 * - Hotel Booking System (Room types, Guest, Reservation)
 * - Sports League Tracker (Team, Player, Match, Standings)
 * - Recipe Social Network (User, Recipe, Review, Rating)
 * - Ticket Booking System (Event, Seat, Booking)
 * - Personal Finance Manager (Account, Transaction, Budget)
 * - E-Commerce Platform (Product, Cart, Order, Customer)
 * 
 * MAKE IT YOURS! Pick something you are passionate about.
 */
public class MyPortfolioApp {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        // TODO: Build your masterpiece!
        System.out.println("=== MY PORTFOLIO APP ===");
        // Start with your class definitions above main
        // Then create objects and demonstrate all features
    }
}
practice_exercises.expected_output:
[Your creative masterpiece goes here!]
This exercise has no fixed output.
Demonstrate all 8 requirements in one impressive program.

hints.message: Step 1: Pick your theme. Step 2: List your classes and their fields. Step 3: List the methods each class needs.
Step 4: Build the simplest version - just classes, constructors, and toString(). Step 5: Add one feature at a time.
Checklist: 3+ classes? Inheritance? ArrayList? 5+ methods? Validation? printf? String ops? Encapsulation? If all yes, you have mastered Java!
